Title: PTPTN announces 4.10pct dividend, highest in 11 years
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Depositors of the National Education Savings Scheme (Simpan SSPN) will receive the highest dividend in 11 years following the National Higher Education Fund Corporation (PTPTN)'s announcement of a 4.10 per cent dividend rate.
Its chairman, Datuk Seri Norliza Abdul Rahim, said the 2025 dividend distribution involves an estimated allocation of RM505.68 million, benefiting 6.07 million depositor accounts. This brings the total dividend paid since the scheme's introduction in 2004 to RM2.63 billion.
She said the dividend will be credited directly into Simpan SSPN accounts, and depositors can check their statements via the myPTPTN application starting tomorrow.
Norliza said total deposits received last year amounted to RM3.33 billion, an increase of 4.86 per cent or RM154.10 million compared with RM3.17 billion in 2024.
"More than 500,000 new accounts were opened in 2025, bringing total deposits as at Dec 31 to RM23.88 billion, and this shows continued strong support from depositors.
"The higher dividend rate reflects PTPTN's efficient fund management, supported by strong governance, integrity and strategic planning to ensure optimal returns for depositors," Norliza said at the Simpan SSPN 2025 dividend announcement ceremony here today.
Also present were PTPTN chief executive Ahmad Dasuki Abdul Majid and its strategic partners.
Norliza noted that the 4.10 per cent dividend is higher than the 4.05 per cent declared for 2024.
She said the increase was supported by short and long-term Syariah-compliant investments in the money market and domestic sukuk, which preserve capital while delivering competitive returns.
"The 4.10 per cent dividend also represents a positive return compared with the Overnight Policy Rate set by Bank Negara Malaysia, which remained at 2.75 per cent throughout 2025.
"Simpan SSPN remains a safe and rewarding education savings platform," she said, expressing appreciation to Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir for his continued support and commending the board of directors and management of PTPTN.
Norliza added that Simpan SSPN is the only savings product offering personal income tax relief of up to RM8,000 per year, along with free takaful protection for depositors and a matching grant of up to RM10,000 for eligible families.
She said the government has also expanded the SSPN Prime matching grant to households earning between RM4,000 and RM6,000 for two years under the Geran Padanan Ihsan (GAPAI) initiative, with grants of up to RM5,000 per family.
In addition, PTPTN is organising the Simpan SSPN 2026 Draw Campaign, offering prizes worth nearly RM1.4 million to 954 winners throughout the year.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bangladeshi worker jailed six months, fined RM15,100 for molesting five women
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A Bangladeshi construction worker who molested five women aged between 21 and 28 at MRT and LRT stations last month was jailed six months and fined RM15,100 by four magistrate's courts today.
Md Romjan Ali, 41, dressed in a blue T-shirt and handcuffed in the dock, was sentenced after he pleaded guilty to five charges read out in Bahasa Malaysia before magistrates Aina Azahra Arifin, Faezahnoor Hasan, Nurul Izzah Hasan Basri and Farah Nabihah Muhamad Dan.
Before Aina Azahra, Romjan was jailed three weeks and fined RM3,500 after admitting to molesting a 21-year-old German woman at 12.40pm along the pedestrian walkway near the LRT Chan Sow Lin station on April 11.
Before Nurul Izzah, he was jailed six months for groping the private parts of a 28-year-old woman in a packed train travelling from the Taman Midah station towards Muzium Negara on April 15.
He was also fined RM3,600 after admitting to molesting a 24-year-old woman at an escalator of Maluri MRT station at 4.45pm on April 25.
Farah Nabihah fined him RM5,000 after he admitted to groping a 28-year-old woman from behind while on a train at 8am at the Maluri MRT station on April 22.
Meanwhile, before Faezahnoor, he was jailed three months and fined RM3,000 for hugging a 25-year-old woman from behind while she was walking near the Chan Sow Lin LRT station at 8.30am on April 10.
In that incident, the victim screamed and fell while trying to escape. She later saw the accused allegedly masturbating.
The courts ordered the jail sentences to run concurrently.
The charges were framed under Section 354 of the Penal Code which carries a maximum 10 year jail sentence or a fine or whipping or both upon conviction.
The prosecution was represented by deputy public prosecutors Hench Goh, Nurainizatul Farhana Zainal and Nur Farhana Mohamad Poad, along with prosecuting officers Inspector M. Alli Nangai and Inspector M. Naresh Kumar, who asked the court to impose a proportionate sentence as a deterrent.
Defence lawyer T. Chandrasegaran sought leniency, saying Romjan was remorseful.
"He apologises for his actions and asks that he be fined," he said.
City police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus had said a Bangladeshi man was arrested at 11.45am on April 27 for using criminal intimidation to molest the victims.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: How an ocean cruise turned into a hantavirus nightmare
Article:
THE first victim of a suspected hantavirus outbreak had already been dead 21 days when fellow passenger Jake Rosmarin posted a video about cows he had seen on a remote volcanic island in the Atlantic, showing no indication he was aware his cruise ship was about to be quarantined.
But later that evening, on May 2, Rosmarin posted: "For those who have seen recent news, yes, I am currently onboard the M/V Hondius," adding that he did not wish to say more, "out of respect for those involved."
The following day, with his ship marooned off the Cape Verde islands and refused permission to offload its passengers and crew, a visibly distraught Rosmarin said, "What's happening right now is very real for all of us."
"We're not just headlines. We're people with families, with lives, with people waiting for us at home," he added, his voice trembling as he choked back tears, a ring on his ring finger visible in the frame.
"All we want is to feel safe and to get home," he said.
That was two days after conveying his excitement at spotting a critically endangered Wilkins's Finch on Nightingale Island.
About 150 people remain stuck on the ship, which had been visiting some of the most remote places on Earth, including Tristan da Cunha, an island in the south Atlantic between Argentina and South Africa where Rosmarin filmed the cows.
Three people on board - a Dutch couple and a German national - have died, the operator said.
Rosmarin did not immediately respond to a request for comment via text message, but his post was a rare insight into the atmosphere on the M/V Hondius.
The first stricken passenger, the Dutch man, died on April 11, as the ship steamed towards Tristan da Cunha.
His body remained on board until April 24, when it "was disembarked on St Helena, with his wife accompanying the repatriation," the ship's operator, Oceanwide Expeditions, said in a statement on Monday.
Three days later, the man's wife also fell sick and later died, while another passenger, a Briton, became "seriously ill and was medically evacuated to South Africa," the company said.
[[nid:1433698]]
South African authorities have confirmed that the British patient, who is being treated in a Johannesburg hospital, tested positive for the hantavirus. The Netherlands has confirmed the virus in the Dutch woman who died.
The Hondius left Ushuaia in southern Argentina in March, according to company documentation, on a voyage marketed as an Antarctic nature expedition, with berth prices ranging from 14,000 to ​22,000 euros (US$16,000-US$25,000).
It travelled past mainland Antarctica, the Falklands, ​South Georgia, Nightingale Island, Tristan, St Helena, ⁠and Ascension before reaching Cape Verdean waters on May 3.
On May 1, the ship's chef Khabir Moraes posted a joyful video of himself and colleagues swimming in the ocean off a rubber dinghy, the cruise ship anchored in the background.
"The day was pleasant and the depth was 4,700 metres," he said, commenting in the video about his colleagues laughing as they hauled him back onto the dinghy.
Moraes did not immediately respond to a Reuters request for comment sent via text message. Reuters could not establish if he was aware of the deaths before he made his post.
The following day, another passenger died, Oceanwide said in Monday's statement, adding that the cause had not yet been established and that the passenger was of German nationality.
Oceanwide Expeditions said Cape Verdean health authorities had not yet granted permission for a medical evacuation of the ship and screening of its passengers.
It is now considering sailing on to Las Palmas or Tenerife for this purpose.
Hantavirus ‌is primarily spread by rodents but can be transmitted between people in rare cases, according to the World Health Organisation.
As concern mounts in South Africa, health authorities said there was no need for locals to be concerned about the virus spreading onshore, while Cape Verde authorities also put out a statement to calm fears, saying that since the ship has remained at sea, "there is currently no risk to the population on land."
* The writer is from Reuters
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor to set up special committee to review school safety following fatal accident
Article:
ISKANDAR PUTERI: The Johor state government will set up a special committee to review school safety following an accident in Batu Pahat yesterday, where an 8-year-old girl was killed after being hit by a van outside her school.
State Education and Information Committee chairman Aznan Tamin said that, apart from investigating the cause of the incident, the special committee will also focus on improving existing safety guidelines and coordinating safety measures at the state level.
"The committee will also support the implementation of the Safe School Campaign, which covers not only road safety but also student safety within school grounds," he told reporters after attending the State Legislative Assembly sitting at the Sultan Ismail Building here today.
Aznan said the district education office (PPD) has been reminded to tighten control over vehicles entering and leaving school premises by ensuring strict compliance with standard operating procedures (SOPs).
All vehicles entering school premises must be registered at the security post and comply with the stipulated guidelines to mitigate risks, he added.
Aznan noted that safety guidelines on vehicle movement in schools were first introduced in 1999 and have been regularly updated to meet current needs.
He said the state government will further streamline these guidelines, particularly through the Safe School Campaign scheduled to be launched early next month, covering student movement, traffic control, and campus safety.
Meanwhile, the Social Welfare Department will provide counselling services to the victim's family and affected students to help them cope with the trauma of the incident.
Aznan added that he will visit the victim's family tomorrow to offer his condolences and provide appropriate assistance.
Yesterday, Nur Aini Umairah Mohd Fareez was killed after being hit by a van in front of Sekolah Kebangsaan (SK) Seri Maimon in Parit Sulong.
Batu Pahat district police chief Assistant Commissioner Shahrulanuar Mushaddat Abdullah Sani said the incident occurred at about 12.30pm in front of the school's main gate.
He said preliminary investigations revealed the accident occurred as a van, which had just delivered goods, was exiting the school's main gate towards Jalan Simpang Lima-Parit Sulong.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pickpocket gang crippled, five members arrested in Melaka
Article:
MELAKA: The activities of a pickpocket syndicate targeting victims at tourist hotspots in the state were crippled when five of its members were arrested last week.
Melaka Tengah district police chief Assistant Commissioner Christopher Patit said the Chinese citizens, aged between 29 and 62, were believed to have operated in a group before targeting victims in crowded areas such as Jonker Walk.
He said the activities of the group, known as "Geng Guangxi', were uncovered after the police received a report from a Chinese tourist on April 25, who claimed to have lost a wallet containing cash and a credit card.
"Acting on information and intelligence, a team from the Melaka Criminal Investigation Department (CID) launched a special operation and successfully detained two suspects around Dataran Pahlawan on the same day.
"Further interrogations led to the arrest of three more suspects at a condominium in Cheras, Kuala Lumpur, on April 29," he said in a statement here today.
Investigations found that the victims were predominantly male tourists, with five cases reported so far involving losses amounting to approximately RM20,000.
Christopher added that during the raids, the police seized various items believed to be the proceeds of criminal activities, including seven mobile phones of various brands, two sling bags, two wallets, foreign identification cards, and various foreign currencies.
He said the case is being investigated under Section 379 of the Penal Code and Section 15(1)(c) of the Immigration Act 1959/63 for theft and overstaying.
"All the suspects are expected to be charged tomorrow at the Ayer Keroh Court," he said.
Following the arrests, he advised the public, especially tourists, to remain vigilant and safeguard their personal belongings when in crowded areas.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: UN demands Israel 'immediately' release Gaza flotilla activists
Article:
GENEVA: The United Nations on Wednesday called on Israel to immediately release two activists taken from a Gaza-bound flotilla, and demanded an investigation into "disturbing accounts" they had been severely mistreated.
Spanish national Saif Abu Keshek and Brazilian Thiago Avila, who are held in a prison in Ashkelon, were among dozens of activists on a Gaza-bound flotilla intercepted by Israeli forces in international waters off the coast of Greece last Thursday.
"Israel must immediately and unconditionally release Global Sumud Flotilla members Saif Abu Keshek and Thiago Avila, who were detained in international waters and brought to Israel where they continue to be held without charge," UN rights office spokesman Thameen Al-Kheetan said in a statement.
"It is not a crime to show solidarity and attempt to bring humanitarian aid to the Palestinian population in Gaza, who are in dire need of it," he insisted.
The flotilla's vessels set sail from France, Spain and Italy with the aim of breaking Israel's blockade of Gaza and delivering humanitarian aid to the war-ravaged Palestinian territory.
Representatives for Avila and Abu Keshek have accused Israeli authorities of abusing the two men, who have been on hunger strike for the past six days.
Kheetan decried the "disturbing accounts of severe mistreatment", calling for an investigation and insisting "those responsible must be brought to justice."
"We call for an end to Israel's use of arbitrary detention and of broadly and vaguely defined terrorism legislation, inconsistent with international human rights law," he said.
"Israel must also end its blockade on Gaza, and allow and facilitate the entry of humanitarian assistance to the besieged Palestinian strip, in sufficient amounts," the spokesman said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ministry raises entry barriers on CBU EVs, ends special exemption
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Investment, Trade and Industry Ministry has tightened the requirements for fully imported (CBU) electric vehicles (EV) following the end of the special exemption for such vehicles.
From July 1, CBU EVs imported into Malaysia must carry a minimum cost, insurance and freight (CIF) value of RM200,000 and deliver at least 180 kW (around 241 hp).
"The ministry  wishes to inform that the special exemption period for the importation of EVs under franchise AP (approved permit) in the form of CBU units for four years ended on Dec 31, 2025.
"Following the end of this exemption period, the policy related to EV CBU has reverted to the existing regulations.
"However, taking into account that companies still have remaining stock, including existing stock, stock at ports, and stock in transit, the ministry allows such stock to be sold in accordance with the provisions under the special exemption period until it is fully depleted," it said in a statement.
The ministry said that this adjustment has been communicated to franchise AP holders through an engagement session held on April 30.
The ministry said that it remains committed to ensuring a transparent, consistent and balanced policy environment in supporting the development of the automotive industry, while safeguarding national economic interests and the rights of vehicle consumers.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bursa Malaysia closes higher on easing geopolitical tensions
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia closed higher today as easing West Asia tensions lifted overall market sentiment and encouraged a broader risk-on tone across regional equities, said an analyst.
At 5 pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) rose 9.44 points, or 0.54 per cent, to 1,756.87 from Tuesday's close of 1,747.43.
The benchmark index, which opened 1.65 points higher at 1,749.08, moved between 1,746.80 and 1,760.30 during the day.
Market breadth was positive with gainers beating losers 785 to 503. A total of 535 counters were unchanged, 809 untraded, and 10 suspended.
Turnover expanded to 3.98 billion units worth RM3.97 billion compared with 2.86 billion units worth RM2.59 billion yesterday.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Flash floods inundate food court in Petaling Jaya
Article:
PETALING JAYA: Heavy rain this afternoon triggered flash floods in an area in Section 51A here, submerging several vehicles in a parking area near a food court.
Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said the department received an emergency call at 4.15pm regarding flooding at a food court along Jalan 51A/223.
[[nid:1433675]]
"A team from the Petaling Jaya fire station arrived at the scene about 11 minutes after the call, with six personnel and a fire rescue tender.
[[nid:1433674]]
"Water levels at the location were estimated to have reached up to four feet," he said in a statement.
He added that several vehicles were found partially submerged in the food court's parking area when firefighters arrived.
[[nid:1433673]]
"No evacuation was carried out as there were no reported victims, and water levels were already receding at the time of operations," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fresh UAE attacks blamed on Iran draw new reality in the Gulf
Article:
HOURS after the UAE's top oil executive said the country had "emerged stronger" from war and as thousands attended an Abu Dhabi summit to boost the local economy, phones across the country were alerting that missiles were incoming.
The first attacks since a truce came into effect last month were a stark reminder of its fragility, dampening hopes of a quick return to normality, even as Iran "categorically" denied on Tuesday that it was behind the new offensive.
The new attacks on Monday, which the UAE blamed on Iran, pushed schools to revert to remote learning, having resumed in-person classes roughly two weeks earlier.
In the weeks preceding the attacks, residents had trickled back to the UAE, where 90 per cent of the population are foreigners, with beachgoers returning to the iconic man-made Palm while Dubai restaurants returned to life.
One executive at a food and beverage company told AFP they were in a meeting to discuss raising salaries following war-driven cuts, when phones began ringing.
"We literally just slammed our faces into our hands and sat in silence for a solid minute," they told AFP, requesting anonymity to discuss sensitive matters.
"There was an overall feeling of... exhaustion, of disbelief that this might start again," they said.
Iran targeted the UAE more than any other country during the war, hitting US assets but also energy and civilian infrastructure and even landmarks.
Despite the UAE's very high interception rate, the attacks broke the aura of stability that the Gulf had long enjoyed and sent tourists fleeing during peak season.
The threat of renewed strikes now looms over the region's wider economy, not just oil and gas, threatening diversification ambitions in the Gulf.
"The UAE non-oil private sector signalled a further loss of momentum in April, with operating conditions showing their weakest performance for more than five years," warned David Owen, a senior economist at S&P Global Market Intelligence.
[[nid:1433669]]
For weeks, Gulf states have been stuck between war and peace as talks stall and the vital Strait of Hormuz remains all but closed.
"This might become a new reality where every now and then we have a few alerts," the executive told AFP, warning the economy depended on stability and the perception of safety.
Speaking about Iran, Emirati political scientist Abdulkhaleq Abdulla said: "Whenever they are angry against America or Israel or anything, they could, they will shoot at us and probably we are their prime target."
The UAE is a top US ally and an Arab country with ties to Israel, making it a prime target for Iran, said HA Hellyer, Middle East expert at the London-based Royal United Services Institute for Defence and Security Studies.
Its diversified economy means Iranian attacks have additional repercussions, while proximity to Iran makes it an easier drone target than Israel, he said.
Iran may also single out the UAE to attempt "to further drive a wedge between Gulf countries", compounding an Emirati-Saudi rift that broke out publicly in December over Yemen.
The Gulf's two biggest economies are divided over the war and on Iran. The UAE has had a more hawkish stance and expressed maximalist demands for any deal, while Saudi Arabia has supported mediator Pakistan's efforts.
The attacks attributed to Iran raise "the risk of Emirati retaliation; Abu Dhabi has signalled it will consolidate further US and Israel ties", Hellyer said.
In contrast, Riyadh has increasingly grown to see Israel as a major threat since normalisation talks fell through following the outbreak of the Gaza war.
Riyadh, which has sustained fewer Iranian attacks than its neighbour, "regards the risks of action as being greater than the risks of inaction and the Emiratis view it in the opposite direction", Hellyer said.
* The writers are from AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RM200 padi aid for farmers needs better targeting, govt told
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Experts have called for better targeting of the RM200 per hectare aid for padi farmers, saying the support is relatively modest as production costs continue to rise.
Universiti Teknologi Mara senior lecturer Dr Mohamad Idham Md Razak said the RM200 assistance is relatively small compared with large-scale subsidies such as fuel support, and is mainly intended to ease farmers' cash flow rather than serve as a long-term solution.
He added that the effectiveness of the aid depends on how well it is targeted, especially to ensure it reaches those most affected by increasing operational expenses.
"The assistance is only meaningful if it helps farmers continue planting despite rising costs. Otherwise, some may scale back operations, which would affect domestic rice supply," he said.
He added that without improvements in targeting and implementation, continued reliance on such assistance could still contribute to fiscal pressure over time.
Putra Business School economic analyst Associate Professor Dr Ida Yasin said rising global oil prices have significantly increased the cost of padi cultivation, particularly for fuel-dependent machinery and related services such as equipment rental.
She added that government support remains necessary as rice prices are regulated to contain inflation, limiting farmers' ability to pass on higher costs.
"If there was no assistance, farmers would be forced to sell at higher prices due to rising costs. Since rice prices are controlled, this support helps bridge the gap," she said.
She added that Malaysia still relies on imports to meet domestic demand, with self-sufficiency levels at about 60 to 65 per cent, making continued support for farmers essential to maintain supply stability.
Recent reports indicate that about 30 per cent of padi farmers may
skip the upcoming planting season
due to rising costs, particularly diesel and machinery expenses, raising concerns over future output.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Evacuations 'ongoing' from hantavirus-hit cruise ship
Article:
PRAIA, Cape Verde: Evacuations were taking place Wednesday from a cruise ship stricken with a deadly outbreak of hantavirus, the World Health Organisation said, as experts confirmed a rare strain that can be transmitted between humans.
Three people – two crew members and one other person – thought to be infected with the virus were being taken off the MV Hondius, anchored off Cape Verde, the WHO said.
"The three of them are stable, and one of the three is asymptomatic," Ann Lindstrand, the WHO representative in Cape Verde, told AFP.
The ship has been at the centre of an international health scare since Saturday, when the UN's health agency was informed that three passengers had died and the suspected cause was hantavirus – a rare disease usually spread from infected rodents typically through urine, droppings and saliva.
The Dutch-flagged cruise ship set sail from Ushuaia in Argentina on April 1 and has been anchored off Cape Verde since Sunday while emergency teams try to deal with a situation.
The ship's Dutch operator Oceanwide Expeditions had said on Tuesday that two seriously ill crew members – one British, one Dutch – and a passenger would be taken off the ship and flown to the Netherlands, allowing the vessel to sail on to Spain's Canary Islands.
Health experts raised concern that a wider outbreak could be on the cards after a Dutch woman with symptoms left the ship and was flown on a passenger plane to Johannesburg, where she later died on April 26.
Efforts are under way to trace people on that flight, which South African-based carrier Airlink said was carrying 82 passengers and six crew.
On Wednesday the Swiss health ministry also confirmed that a cruise ship passenger had been hospitalised with hantavirus in Zurich.
South Africa's health minister Aaron Motsoaledi told a parliament committee on Wednesday that tests had found the Andes strain, the only one that can be passed between humans.
"But as we said, we want to repeat again, such transmission is very rare and only happens due to very close contact between people," the minister said.
The Geneva University Hospital also confirmed it was the Andes strain, adding that it was behind all three deaths from the ship.
Some passengers and crew have been in isolation after Cape Verde authorities barred the ship from docking. The ship is anchored just off the island nation's capital Praia.
Spain's health ministry said on Tuesday the ship was due to arrive in the Canaries in "three to four days", adding that the island chain was the closest place with the necessary facilities.
The cruise counted 88 passengers and 59 crew members, with 23 nationalities onboard, the WHO said.
Two hantavirus cases have been confirmed – including one of the fatalities and a British passenger currently in intensive care in Johannesburg – with five further suspected cases, the WHO said earlier.
The WHO was trying to work out how hantavirus had appeared on the ship, with the first person who died having developed symptoms on April 6.
Human-to-human transmission has only been reported in previous outbreaks of one specific hantavirus called Andes virus, which circulates in South America.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Cambodia says strife in Southeast Asia could crimp energy crisis response
Article:
BANGKOK: Southeast Asia's joint effort to tackle global issues such as an energy crisis caused by the Iran war is complicated by internal friction over tension between Thailand and Cambodia and civil war in Myanmar, a senior Cambodian diplomat said on Wednesday.
Asia is the world's top oil importing region and the Iran war has sent many of the 11 members of the Asean bloc scrambling for alternatives to insulate their economies from the worst of the energy crisis triggered by the Middle East conflict.
"Asean needs to put our house in order and sort things out as soon as possible," Cambodia's envoy to the Association of Southeast Asian Nations, Kung Phoak, told Reuters ahead of a summit of the bloc in the Philippines this week.
The Southeast Asian conflicts will figure in discussions at a leaders' summit on Friday, as Asean members push for the ratification of an oil-sharing deal to strengthen regional resilience in energy.
"The sooner we can get all these problems resolved, I think it enhances our ability and gives us more time to think about what we can do more, and not just to respond to the (energy) crisis but also to prepare Asean for the future," he added.
The energy crisis is accelerating efforts to ratify and put into operation the Asean Petroleum Security Agreement that aims to boost members' coordination to protect fuel supplies, he said.
Many governments in the region have moved to limit fuel use, conserve energy or clamp down on ​hoarding, while several have curbed exports and launched diplomatic efforts to ensure access.
Asean's unity is challenged by the grinding civil war raging in Myanmar since a 2021 coup, and tension still simmering between Thailand and Cambodia despite a December ceasefire in their deadly border war.
Four months after the neighbours signed a truce, Kung Phoak said they had failed to make headway on holding talks to demarcate sections of their land border, with Thai forces still occupying some Cambodian territory.
"Leaving the situation lingering like this is not in the benefit of either of us," he said, adding that the Thai government had not responded to Cambodian requests for a meeting within a framework to negotiate border issues.
Kung Phoak, who is Cambodia's secretary of state for foreign affairs and international cooperation, also decried a Thai move on Tuesday to end a decades-old pact aimed at resolving disputes over potentially oil-rich maritime areas.
Still, he said he hoped the maritime disputes could be quickly resolved through a process under the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea.
Thailand's foreign ministry did not immediately respond to questions from Reuters, but its government said on Tuesday it was still committed to negotiations.
Kung Phoak also welcomed the transfer of Myanmar's ousted leader Aung San Suu Kyi to house arrest by the military-backed government, saying he hoped it imparts positive momentum to resolve the civil war that followed a 2021 coup.
On Tuesday, Thailand's foreign minister told Reuters he wanted Myanmar's new foreign minister to brief Asean in the near future, while the Philippines urged it to allow the bloc's special envoy to meet Suu Kyi.
"The general mood of Asean member states at the moment is that people also want to look at what's next ... because when we are talking with Myanmar people they are yearning for peace, stability and improvement of their well-being," Kung Phoak said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: NPE's Kuchai Link closed as heavy rain triggers flash floods, traffic gridlock reported
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Heavy downpour in the Klang Valley this afternoon has triggered flash floods around the city.
New Pantai Expressway (NPE) highway operator in a posting on X said the inclement weather had forced a full closure along the Kuchai Link stretch from Sungai Besi towards Jalan Klang Lama.
"Traffic is reported to be moving slowly for up to two kilometres approaching the incident location as vehicles are redirected away from the flooded section.
"Motorists have been advised to avoid the route after unexpected flooding disrupted traffic flow. The NPE Ronda patrol team is currently on site assisting with traffic control and diversion efforts," it said.
It also reported left and middle lane closures of the NPE Highway at Km18.9 at the Maju Jaya U-turn eastbound towards Bangsar due to flash flooding
Meanwhile, based on the City Hall Kuala Lumpur Command and Control Centre (KLC3) closed-circuit television cameras (CCTV), flash floods and stagnant water near Desa Tun Razak along the Middle Ring Road Two (MRR2) have added to congestion from Lotus's Ampang.
The New Straits Times have reached out to the City Fire and Rescue Department for updates.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Four Negri Sembilan BN-held exco posts remain vacant for now, says MB
Article:
SEREMBAN: The four Negri Sembilan executive committee positions that became vacant following the move by Barisan Nasional assemblymen to withdraw support for Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun will remain vacant for now.
Aminuddin said the state government has yet to make a decision on the status of the vacant posts.
He said for the time being, the six remaining state executive councillors will oversee the portfolios previously held by the four Umno assemblymen.
"No decision has been made regarding replacements for the vacant posts. However, specific meetings or field duties will be handled by the existing exco members.
"We are currently listing these tasks. Insya-Allah, they will be discussed with the other council members from time to time.
"We need a bit more time regarding the appointment to fill the vacant positions," he said after chairing the weekly state executive council meeting at Wisma Negeri today.
The
four vacant positions
were held by Negri Sembilan Umno liaison committee chairman Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias, who is also Pertang assemblyman; Senaling assemblyman Datuk Ismail Lasim; Datuk Mustapha Nagoor (Palong assemblyman); and Datuk Mohd Faizal Ramli (Linggi assemblyman).
When asked if any Umno assemblymen had contacted him following the resolution reached by the Umno-pakatan harapan leadership consultative council last night, Aminuddin replied in the negative.
Regarding the postponed state legislative assembly sitting, the state pakatan harapan chairman said a new date has yet to be determined.
"We are currently reviewing several documents and bills that are to be tabled.
"It is possible the sitting will be held concurrently with the presentation of the state budget," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Human-to-human hantavirus strain confirmed in cruise passenger: S. Africa
Article:
JOHANNESBURG: The Andes strain of the hantavirus that is transmissible between humans has been confirmed in a passenger evacuated to South Africa from a stricken cruise ship, the health minister said Wednesday.
Three people from the cruise ship have died, one of them in Johannesburg after she was transferred on a commercial flight from the Atlantic island of Saint Helena.
Another passenger, a British national, was evacuated separately and is in a Johannesburg hospital.
"The preliminary tests show that, indeed, this is the Andes strain," South Africa's health minister Aaron Motsoaledi told a parliament committee.
"And it happens to be the only strain out of the 38 that is known to cause human-to-human transmission.
"But as we said, we want to repeat again, such transmission is very rare and only happens due to very close contact between people."
WHO has said there are two confirmed and five suspected cases of the virus.
South African authorities are tracing nearly 90 people who were on the flight that transported a 69-year-old Dutch woman who died in Johannesburg after her husband, 70, died of the virus on the ship.
She had left the ship at Saint Helena, a remote island in the South Atlantic, with "gastrointestinal symptoms" on April 24.
She died at the emergency department of a Johannesburg hospital on April 26 after her condition deteriorated during the flight, the World Health Organisation said.
"We need to know who were the people who were in contact with this lady," Motsoaledi said.
Besides the plane passengers, authorities were tracing people at the airport where she arrived and at the hospital where she was admitted, he said.
The South African-based carrier Airlink that operated the flight that transported the woman told AFP Tuesday that 82 passengers and six crew were on board the April 25 flight.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Rafizi: MACC probe focuses on procedure, not money flows
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Former Economy Minister Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli said investigations into him have so far focused on procedural matters rather than financial transactions regarding the RM1.1 billion semiconductor deal between the Economy Ministry and UK-based firm Arm Holdings.
Speaking to reporters outside the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) headquarters today, he said no questions during his statement recording touched on money flows or payments, contrary to earlier claims.
"There were no questions on financial transactions. As I have said, the questions so far have been about joint venture decisions and procedures," he said.
Rafizi arrived at the MACC headquarters at 1.50pm to continue giving his statement for a third day, assisting in the investigation into alleged abuse of power and misappropriation linked to the deal.
The MACC opened the investigation on Feb 16 following complaints from several non-governmental organisations alleging the deal was rushed and skewed, potentially resulting in financial implications for the government.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Suspect in assault of pregnant wife expected to be charged tomorrow
Article:
SUNGAI PETANI: A man who assaulted his wife until she miscarried last month will be charged at the Sungai Petani Court tomorrow.
Kuala Muda district police chief Assistant Commissioner Hanyan Ramlan confirmed the matter today.
Rosmaini Abd Raof, 43, will be charged under Section 326 of the Penal Code for voluntarily causing grievous hurt with dangerous weapons or means.
It was previously reported that the woman suffered injuries to several parts of her body after being beaten by her husband, which also caused her to miscarry a 12-week-old foetus.
The victim, who is a civil servant, is still receiving treatment for her injuries at the Sultan Abdul Halim Hospital (HSAH).
She was allegedly
punched, kicked, and struck
with a clothes hanger, a rubber hose, and a metal curtain rod, resulting in fractured left and right ribs, a fractured spine, a miscarriage, and swelling and bruises all over her body.
On Monday, Rosmaini was sentenced to 14 years in jail for a previous domestic assault case, which had left his pregnant wife in a coma.
The wife, Jahidah Nordin @ Sylvester, 48, is now in a vegetative state, while her son, whom she gave birth to via caesarean section while still in a coma on Nov 30, 2021, is experiencing developmental issues and is certified as disabled.
The court also ordered Rosmaini, 43, to
pay RM25,000 in compensatio
n to Jahidah through her younger sister, Fazariah, or face an additional six months in jail if he fails to do so.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: AI and the strategic importance of information resilience
Article:
ARTIFICIAL intelligence (AI) is changing the way information is generated, disseminated and consumed. In the contemporary digital environment, information has become a central arena of strategic competition.
A state may have strong institutions, a resilient economy and capable security forces; yet it can still be weakened if public trust erodes, official information is challenged, or people become vulnerable to hostile information campaigns.
Artificial intelligence has intensified this challenge. AI-enabled systems can rapidly generate, analyse and disseminate text, images, audio and video at scale.
They can identify target audiences, track public sentiment and reactions, and tailor messages to evoke emotions and shape perceptions.
Information campaigns can therefore be conducted not only with greater speed and scale, but with greater precision.
Hostile information campaigns and disinformation efforts are no longer limited to single messages, isolated falsehoods or one-off attempts to mislead.
They can operate continuously across multiple digital platforms. Campaign actors can observe how audiences respond, modify messages, and rapidly push revised narratives to deepen emotional reactions.
The strategic danger today lies not only in false information, but in the gradual and insidious erosion of trust.
When people are repeatedly bombarded with misleading narratives, manipulated content or emotionally charged claims, they may gradually begin to doubt official information, institutional policies and even credible evidence.
In such an environment, truth competes not only against falsehood, but also against confusion, cynicism and public exhaustion from repeated claims and counter-claims.
When trust is weakened, even accurate communication may struggle to gain acceptance. Once uncertainty spreads, response efforts become harder to coordinate.
Social cohesion may weaken, with possible effects on domestic security.
Policy choices may become more contested while decision-makers may face pressure to respond before facts are clear.
Small and middle powers are especially exposed to these risks.
Their vulnerability lies in how much disruption their systems can absorb, how exposed they are to external information flows, and how limited their response options may be.
Smaller national systems may have fewer resources to detect, analyse and counter sustained information campaigns.
Open societies and digital economies are highly exposed to external information flows and shocks. At the same time, governments may face limits in technical capability, strategic communication capacity and institutional coordination.
This does not mean that small and middle powers are helpless or defenceless. It simply means that resilience must be built before a national crisis occurs.
The effectiveness of hostile information campaigns depends not only on the capability of the perpetrator, but also on the preparedness of the target society.
States with stronger public trust, clearer communication structures and stronger coordination mechanisms are more likely to withstand hostile information campaigns.
Information resilience should therefore be understood as a whole-of-government and whole-of-society challenge.
It involves government institutions, technology platforms, media organisations, educators, civil society and citizens. Each has a role in shaping how information is verified, trusted and shared.
Several areas deserve attention. First, strategic communication must be credible, timely and coordinated. During crises, silence or inconsistency creates space for speculation and manipulation.
Governments need clear ownership of messaging, unity across agencies and the ability to communicate directly with domestic and external audiences.
Credibility cannot be manufactured on demand during a crisis. It is built over time through consistent honesty and competence.
Second, public communication efforts need to be clear and aligned across government. Information threats often operate across public and private systems.
Digital platforms, media organisations and state agencies all influence the information environment.
Clear rules, responsibilities and coordination mechanisms can help ensure that harmful manipulation is addressed without undermining basic human rights, public trust or the rule of law.
Third, technological safeguards matter. States and societies need better tools to detect manipulated content, identify coordinated behaviour and verify whether information is authentic.
Detection tools must be supported by public communication, institutional processes and trusted channels for clarification.
Fourth, societal resilience is essential. Citizens are active participants in the information environment.
Governments should therefore educate citizens to question sensational claims and avoid forwarding unverified content. A prepared population is harder to manipulate.
Information resilience is not about censorship or suppressing public discourse but strengthening media literacy, public awareness and critical thinking so that society is less vulnerable to harmful falsehoods and manipulation.
The challenge for states is not to counter every false narrative, but to build societies that can absorb, question and recover from information shocks.
In the digital age, resilience is not only about protecting systems and institutions. It is also about protecting the cognitive space, where trust, sound judgement and social cohesion reside.
For small and middle powers, the strategic lesson is clear: future crises may begin with contests over  perception, manipulated content and the gradual erosion of confidence.
For this reason, building information resilience early is central to national resilience.
* The writer is a colonel in the Singapore Armed Forces and currently attending the National Resilience College at the National Centre for Defence Studies (PUSPAHANAS) Putrajaya
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: NGO lodges report against Negri Sembilan Umno chief over remarks on state ruler
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A non-governmental organisation is calling on police to investigate statements made by Negri Sembilan Umno chairman Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias for allegedly touching on the sensitivities and authority of the state's Yang di-Pertuan Besar.
The police report was lodged by the chairman of the Taat Setia Sokongan Yang di-Pertuan Besar Negeri Sembilan Secretariat, Datuk Seri Megat D Shahriman Zaharudin, for the statement allegedly said on May 2.
"This report is made to ask authorities to probe an incident on May 2, 2026, which we believe has touched on the sensitivity and authority of the Yang di-Pertuan Besar Negri Sembilan.
"The secretariat is of the view that the royal institution must be preserved from any action that can affect its position being a constitutional monarchy that is impartial to partisan politics in line with the Negri Sembilan's Constitution and the Federal Constitution," he said in a statement today.
Megat D Shahriman said that he was confident that police will take action and investigate, ending his statement with "#tangkapjalal."
At a press conference this morning, after lodging a report at the Dang Wangi police station, he also said that royalty related matters should be separated from politics.
Last week, 14 Umno assemblymen withdrew their confidence from Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun.
During Umno's 80-year convention last Saturday, Jalaluddin allegedly spoke about the events surrounding the crisis and why he and the other assemblymen withdrew their support for the state's Menteri Besar.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sabah to leverage on advanced tech to strengthen security
Article:
LAHAD DATU: Sabah will enhance security capabilities through the use of advanced technology, including artificial intelligence to ensure stability as the foundation of the state's economic growth.
Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor said among the initiatives were the use of technologies such as drones, radar, and big data analysis, to ensure more comprehensive monitoring and faster responses to threats.
"In line with the Hala Tuju Sabah Maju Jaya (SMJ) 2.0 framework, we want security agencies to strengthen the use of AI in operations so that our security system is not only physically strong, but also digitally resilient.
"Without strong security guarantees, investor confidence will be affected, economic activities will be hampered, and the well-being of the people will also suffer," he said in a statement after a security inspection in the Eastern Sabah Security Zone (EssZone) at the Dewan Perdana here.
Hajiji gave his assurances that the welfare of security personnel will continue to be safeguarded through cooperation between the state and federal governments, including in terms of facilities, housing and logistics.
Meanwhile, he said the maritime sector and Blue Economy have been identified as new growth drivers under SMJ 2.0 encompassing the modern fisheries industry, marine tourism, and the development of coastal and border towns.
Hajiji added that such a huge potential can only be fully realised if the security of the state's waters continues to be strengthened without compromise.
In the context of Lahad Datu, he said the district has significant economic potential, particularly in the plantation, fisheries, tourism and downstream industries sectors.
"Sabah holds a strategic position with a coastline stretching over 1,400km and serves as the nation's frontline in Borneo and the Sulu Sea.
"Strengthening security not only protects the people, but also opens opportunities for the development of border towns as new economic gateways for the state," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Negri Sembilan Umno stays in state government, maintains no-confidence in Aminuddin
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Negri Sembilan Umno will remain in the state unity government but will not withdraw its no-confidence stance against Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun.
State Umno secretary Datuk Mustapha Nagoor said all 14 Barisan Nasional assemblymen will continue to carry out their duties in the state government following a decision reached during the Umno-Pakatan Harapan Leadership Consultative Council meeting last night.
However, he said the party maintains its position of having lost confidence in Aminuddin over what it described as a failure to manage the worst crisis in the state.
"We respect the decision of the leadership to maintain cooperation with the Negri Sembilan unity government, as decided by the Umno Political Bureau and the Umno-PH Leadership Consultative Council.
"However, we maintain our loss of confidence in the menteri besar over the failure to manage the worst crisis ever to occur in this state, which has affected public confidence in the adat institutions and the Negri Sembilan Constitution," he said in a statement.
Mustapha said the party also hoped the ongoing leadership and Adat-related crisis in the state would be resolved in a peaceful and orderly manner, in accordance with the Negri Sembilan Constitution 1959 and proper customary practices.
"We urge the public to respect the ongoing processes and to avoid making provocative remarks or statements that could insult or undermine the state's adat institutions," he said.
Umno and PH last night agreed to
maintain their cooperation in the Negri Sembilan unity government
, affirming their commitment to the state's stability and the welfare of its people.
The council also agreed on a mechanism to handle potentially contentious issues in the future.
Last week, 14 Umno assemblymen
withdrew
their support for Aminuddin.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Rural Sabah teacher earns global Cambridge recognition for inclusive learning efforts
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A Malaysian teacher has been named a regional winner of the 2026 Cambridge Dedicated Teacher Awards for advancing inclusive education for neurodivergent learners in rural Sabah.
Assistant principal and Special Educational Needs (SEN) teacher Dr Velerie Wheelervon Primus of Sekolah Kebangsaan Bingkor in Sabah was recognised for his efforts in transforming the SEN sector.
"I believe education is not just about knowledge, but about restoring dignity and creating a sense of belonging," Velerie said.
As one of nine regional winners globally, Velerie will receive £500 (approximately RM2,682) worth of books for his class, a trophy and international recognition.
He will also be featured in a "Thank You" page in selected Cambridge textbooks from November 2026.
Velerie oversees individual education plans, mentors educators and works closely with families to support neurodivergent students.
He also developed the NeuroArts Framework, which integrates therapeutic practices, nature-based learning and the arts into education.
He was nominated by his school principal, who described him as a transformative leader in inclusive education and advocacy for neurodivergent children.
Voting is now open for the global winner of the 2026 Cambridge Dedicated Teacher Awards, with the public invited to support Dr Velerie Wheelervon Primus or one of the eight other regional winners.
Voting closes at 4pm Malaysian time on May 13, while the overall winner will be announced on June 2.
Cambridge International Education group managing director Rod Smith said Velerie's work highlighted the value of inclusive learning.
"By standing alongside neurodivergent learners and their families, often in challenging communities, he has shown how compassion and belief in every child can transform lives.
"That is why he is a deserving recipient," he said, adding that all winners served as an inspiration to educators worldwide.
The statement noted that the 2026 edition attracted more than 12,000 nominations from 126 countries, reflecting growing recognition of teachers' contributions not only in the classroom but also to students' well-being.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Utility issues and logistical challenges slow Sabah Pan Borneo Highway works
Article:
KINABATANGAN: Several major constraints have been affecting Pan Borneo Highway projects in Sabah, said Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor.
He said the Sabah Pan Borneo Highway Project Phase 1B is among the government's strategic initiatives to strengthen the road infrastructure network, thereby boosting economic growth and connecting key areas on Sabah's east coast.
"Utility-related problems were identified as among the main factors slowing earthworks, in addition to logistical challenges and temporary access issues within the project area.
"The state government is committed to ensuring that every development project is implemented in a well-planned manner, closely monitored and completed according to schedule for the benefit of the people," he said in a statement following visits to Sandakan and Lahad Datu.
Hajiji had inspected the WP25 project, which spans 14.96km from Kampung Perpaduan Datuk Moh to Sukau.
To date, the project's physical progress is about 12.35 per cent, compared to the original scheduled target of 15.30 per cent, while financial progress is 11.52 per cent.
The project, valued at about RM269.9 million, is scheduled for completion within 36 months, with a target date of Feb 2028.
Apart from WP25, Hajiji was also briefed on the progress of the nearby WP24 package, which involves a 15.88km stretch from Sungai Takala to Kampung Perpaduan Datuk Moh.
The Pan Borneo Highway Sabah
project
was launched on April 24, 2016. It was initially scheduled for completion in 2021.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Negri Sembilan government no longer minority administration, says Aminuddin
Article:
SEREMBAN: The Negri Sembilan unity government is no longer a minority government, Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun said.
The Sikamat assemblyman said this follows the consensus reached during the Umno-Pakatan Harapan Leadership Consultative Council meeting last night, in which both Umno and PH agreed to maintain cooperation in the Negri Sembilan unity government.
Aminuddin, who is also PKR vice-president, said the state government has now returned to its original status, effectively resolving the recent leadership and political crisis.
"There is no longer a minority government status now that the Umno and PH top leadership have decided to maintain cooperation within the unity government.
"The term 'minority' is no longer appropriate based on yesterday's political bureau decision.
"Furthermore, two opposition (Bersatu) assemblymen have stated they no longer support the move (by the 14 Umno assemblymen) to withdraw support.
"My focus now is simply on getting back to work," he said after chairing the weekly state executive councillor meeting at Wisma Negeri today.
Aminuddin also said he accepted the decision made by the central leadership and will abide by the issued directives.
"I accept the decision and will carry out the mandate given to me," he said.
It was previously reported that Negri Sembilan descended into a leadership crisis after all
Umno and Barisan Nasional assemblymen withdrew
their support for Aminuddin as the menteri besar.
They cited the crisis involving the Adat Perpatih customary system as the reason why they lost confidence in Aminuddin's leadership.
However, Aminuddin had maintained that he would continue his duties despite the 14 BN assemblymen withdrawing their support.
He had said then that the state government would function as a minority government following the decree of the Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir.
On other matters, Aminuddin said the state government would not interfere in customary matters within the luak, including the appointment of the new Undang of Luak Sungei Ujong.
This stance also applies to the legal challenge involving five individuals who filed an originating summons to suspend the enforcement of decisions made during the special meeting of the Negri Sembilan Dewan Keadilan dan Undang (DKU) on April 17.
"We have received the originating summons and have left further action to the due process of law.
"In truth, we do not interfere at the customary or luak levels," he said.
It was reported yesterday that Datuk Mubarak Thahak, who was previously dismissed from his position as the Undang of Luak Sungei Ujong, has, along with five others, filed an
originating summons t
o suspend the enforcement of a decision made during a special meeting of the Negri Sembilan DKU on April 17.
The other five plaintiffs in the suit are the Undang of Luak Jelebu, Datuk Maarof Mat Rashad; the Undang of Luak Johol, Datuk Muhammed Abdullah; the Undang of Luak Rembau, Datuk Abdul Rahim Yasin; the Tunku Besar of Tampin, Tunku Syed Razman Tunku Syed Idrus Al-Qadri; and the Shahbandar of Sungei Ujong, Datuk Seri Badarudin Abdul Khalid.
They have named the DKU, the DKU's secretary, Raja Norazli Raja Nordin, and the Negeri Sembilan state government as defendants.
Through the summons, they are seeking a declaration that as members of the DKU, they are entitled to be provided with a copy of the minutes from the DKU special session held on April 17 at Istana Besar Seri Menanti.
The plaintiffs are requesting an order for the first and second defendants, either by themselves or their agents, to produce and deliver the minutes of the special session to them within seven days.
They have requested an order to suspend the implementation of any enforcement by the defendants related to the decision of the special DKU meeting dated April 17 until the said minutes are furnished to them.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Contraflow lane to start on Senai-JB corridor ahead of RTS Link ops
Article:
ISKANDAR PUTERI: A contraflow lane will be implemented along the Senai-Johor Baru corridor, particularly Jalan Skudai, to address critical traffic congestion following the commencement of the Johor Baru-Singapore Rapid Transit System Link operations expected next year.
Johor Public Works, Transport, Infrastructure and Communications Committee chairman Mohamad Fazli Mohamad Salleh said the measure would be carried out on a three-month trial basis, with implementation targeted in the third quarter of this year, subject to approval from the relevant authorities.
"This (approach) will in turn reduce congestion and daily travel time as a practical interim solution," he said during the oral question-and-answer session at the Johor Legislative Assembly sitting at Bangunan Sultan Ismail here today.
He said the first phase of the contraflow lane implementation would involve a 6km stretch from Batu 7 Jalan Skudai to Bandar Baru Uda, specifically for traffic heading into the city centre.
"The lane will operate during peak hours from 5.30am to 9am and will only involve light vehicles, using a movable barrier system supported by LED signboards and on-ground traffic control to ensure safety and smooth operations," he said.
Fazli said a data-driven approach had been adopted in assessing preparedness for the traffic flow increase following the RTS Link operations, including through the "Greater Johor Baru & JS-SEZ Traffic Dispersal and Congestion Action Plan" study conducted via the Johor Public Transport Corporation (PAJ).
He said the study identified 77 congestion hotspots grouped into 41 clusters, with 24 clusters categorised as serious in Johor Bahru, Pasir Gudang, Iskandar Puteri and Kulai.
In this regard, he said various mitigation measures had been and were being implemented, which would also include proposed priority projects under the Skudai corridor involving road upgrades and road closures in several areas.
To ensure comprehensive RTS Link connectivity, he said Bas.my Johor would serve as the main route, while Bas Muafakat Johor would operate as feeder services in key residential areas in Kota Iskandar, with the first-mile and last-mile approach to include pedestrian facilities, cycling lanes and park-and-ride facilities.
On the issue of cargo lorry congestion on roads, he said logistic companies are encouraged to use the 39.9km Kempas Baru-Pasir Gudang Cargo Service Rail Line, which connect Kempas Baru, Seelong and Johor Port in Pasir Gudang and had been upgraded at a total cost of RM55 million.
He added that route coordination, logistics traffic and daily traffic separation, special routes designation and lorry movement schedules coordination with border and port operations were also implemented to address heavy vehicle congestion issues.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Court dismisses Ratu Naga's bid to refer questions of law to Federal Court
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The High Court today dismissed Syarul Ema Rena Abu Samah's bid to refer two questions of law to the Federal Court over being denied legal representation during questioning by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC).
Judge Mahazan Mat Taib made the ruling on the grounds that the facts as to whether Syarul Ema Rena, also known as Ratu Naga, was detained or not remain disputed and require a full trial.
Therefore, the court ruled that Syarul Ema Rena's application did not meet the threshold under Section 84 of the Courts of Judicature Act 1964.
The court then set May 11 for an e-Review case management to schedule the trial date.
Today's proceedings were attended by lawyer Latheefa Koya representing Syarul Ema Rena, while senior federal counsel Nur Irmawatie Daud represented the Attorney General.
On Jan 14, Syarul Ema Rena filed an application to refer two questions of law under Section 84 of the Courts of Judicature Act 1964.
The first was whether Sections 28A(3), (8) and (9) of the Criminal Procedure Code are void or unlawful or unconstitutional for contravening Article 5(3) of the Federal Constitution.
The second was whether restricting a detained person's access to a lawyer under Sections 28A(3), (8) and (9) of the Criminal Procedure Code is disproportionate to police investigation needs and therefore violates Article 8(1) of the Federal Constitution.
Syarul Ema Rena filed an originating summons on Feb 26, 2024, naming MACC chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki, MACC and the Government of Malaysia as the first to third defendants.
She sought a declaration that she has a legal right under Article 5 of the Constitution (liberty of the person) to be represented by a lawyer when being examined orally and questioned under Section 30(1)(a) of the MACC Act 2009.
She sought a further declaration that the denial of her right to a lawyer is unlawful and unconstitutional for violating Articles 5 and 8 (equality before the law) of the Constitution.
—Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Gombak welfare home operator pleads not guilty to carnal intercourse charges against minor
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The operator of an unlicensed children's welfare home in Gombak was hauled to court for the third time today to face two charges of committing carnal intercourse against the nature of order on a 15-year-old boy three years ago.
The fresh charges at the Kuala Lumpur Sessions Court bring the total he faces to 11, following four counts of physical and non-physical sexual abuse at the Ampang Sessions Court.
At the Selayang Sessions Court earlier, he pleaded not guilty to four charges of abusing four children under his care and another count for operating the unregistered home.
During proceedings today, S. Valan, 31, who appeared in the dock wearing a white round neck T-shirt, entered his plea before Sessions Court judge Suhaila Haron.
"I understand the charges. I plead not guilty," he said as his wife and parents observed proceedings from the public gallery.
On both counts, he is accused of committing the offences against the boy between 11pm and 2am on Jan 2, 2023; and at 2pm on Nov 22, 2023 in the bedroom of a condominium unit in Segambut.
The charges framed under Sections 377B and Section 377C of the Penal Code carry a maximum 20-year jail term and whipping; and a jail term of between five and 20 years and whipping respectively upon conviction.
Deputy public prosecutor Md Subri Hashim did not offer bail for the accused.
"These charges are non-bailable, therefore, I am not proposing bail.
"However, if the court exercises its discretion to grant bail, it should be set at RM20,000 with one surety for each charge.
"The accused should also surrender his passport, refrain from harrassing witnessses and report to the police station monthly until the case is disposed of.
"The court must consider that the victim was a minor at the time of the alleged offences and had been entrusted to the accused's care," he said.
However, defence lawyer Nur Amalia Md Daud asked the court to grant reasonable bail, citing the principle that her client is innocent until proven guilty.
"His mother, a housewife, is present in court and prepared to post bail if allowed.
"The accused is married, has a three-year-old child and lives with his family in Sentul. He is not a flight risk.
"He no longer has access to the victim or the welfare home. If there are concerns about interference with witnesses, the court can impose additional conditions," she said.
She said the Ampang and Selayang Sessions Courts had granted bail, with his passport already impounded.
After hearing submissions from both parties, Suhaila granted bail of RM9,000 with one surety for each charge and set June 8 for mention.
She also ordered the accused not to harass prosecution witnesses and to report to the police station monthly until the case is concluded.
Lawyers Kirthen Subramaniam and Nishanthi Loganathan held a watching brief on behalf of the victim's families and Pertubuhan Kebangkitan Tiga Tangan Selangor, which exposed the alleged decade long abuse at the unlicensed home.
Two former residents, who had just turned 18, came forward with allegations of abuse.
Last month, the home's caretaker,
Retna Velu, 25, pleaded guilty
to 15 charges of sexual assault and committing carnal intercourse against the order of nature involving five boys, the youngest aged 8.
He will be sentenced on May 21 at the Ampang Sessions Court.
The New Straits Times had reported that at least 12 out of 36 occupants of the home had been subjected to abuse.
Medical examination conducted also showed old and new injuries aligned with the alleged sexual abuse of some male children.
Police arrested and remanded four suspects, including the home's 31-year-old owner and another three workers aged between 17 and 25.
The home was not registered with the Social Welfare Department and had instead been operating under a private company.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Teen charged with dangerous driving causing cyclist's death
Article:
KUCHING: A teenage boy pleaded not guilty in the magistrate's court here today to a charge of dangerous driving causing the death of a cyclist on May 1.
Elijah Jeremiah Yeo Shi Xian, 19, made the plea before magistrate Nursyaheeqa Nazwa Radzali.
He was accused of driving dangerously and colliding with a cyclist, causing death, at Jalan Stutong here at 5.30am on May 1.
The charge was brought under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987, which provides for imprisonment of not less than five years and not more than ten years, as well as a maximum fine of RM50,000.
In addition, a conviction would also lead to disqualification from holding a driving licence for a period of not less than five years and cancellation of the licence if holding a probationary driving licence.
The court allowed the accused to be released on bail of RM20,000 with two sureties, and set June 5 for case management.
The prosecution was conducted by deputy public prosecutor Mohd Fillanny Siji, while the accused was represented by lawyer Daniel Ling.
On May 1, Kuching police chief ACP Alexson Naga Chabu reported that a male cyclist had died after being struck by a vehicle in an early morning accident on Jalan Stutong.
Police, who received a report regarding the accident at 5.43am, said the victim, aged 56, sustained severe head injuries and was pronounced dead at the scene.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] PM orders coordinated action on unregulated houses of worship
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has directed several ministries and agencies to coordinate efforts to address issues involving unregulated houses of worship.
Government spokesman Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the National Unity Ministry had been tasked with leading discussions involving the Housing and Local Government Ministry, Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Ministry, Home Ministry, the Federal Territories Department and other relevant stakeholders.
He said the discussions were aimed at finding solutions to issues involving unregulated houses of worship nationwide.
On Feb 9, Anwar said the government
would not allow the construction of unregulated houses of worship.
He said all houses of worship must comply with the law, adding that local authorities were empowered to remove structures built without proper legal approval.
The Sultan of Selangor,
Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah,
has also called for all houses of worship to be built only after proper applications are submitted and lawful approval obtained from the government and local authorities in accordance with relevant laws and guidelines.
The Selangor Royal Office said unresolved issues involving unregulated houses of worship had long caused concern among communities and affected peace and harmony in the state.
The Ruler said the principle of lawful and orderly construction must apply to all houses of worship, particularly Hindu temples, to ensure development is properly planned, sustainable and aligned with urban regulations.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Flash floods hit parts of Klang Valley after heavy rain (VIDEO)
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 6 — Heavy rain since 4pm today has triggered flash floods in several areas across the Klang Valley, disrupting traffic flow.
A spokesperson for the Malaysian Highway Authority (LLM) said the agency received reports of flash flooding at KM48.0 of the Shah Alam Expressway (KESAS) heading from Kinrara towards Awan Besar, causing congestion stretching up to four kilometres since 4.27pm, according to national news agency Bernama.
Flooding was also reported along the Kuchai Link towards the New Pantai Expressway, as well as at KM18.9 towards Maju Jaya, resulting in slow-moving traffic and one lane being inundated.
A food court along Jalan 51a/223, Section 51A in Petaling Jaya inundated with flood water. — Pictures from X/Bernama
Meanwhile, Selangor Fire and Rescue Department operations assistant director Ahmad Mukhlis Mokhtar said the department received a report of flash flooding at a food court along Jalan 51a/223, Section 51A in Petaling Jaya at 4.15pm.
“The flash flood at the food court area was estimated at 121 centimetres, with several vehicles submerged in the parking area.
“No evacuations were carried out and water levels are receding,” he said in a statement today.
He added that six personnel from the Petaling Jaya Fire and Rescue Station were deployed to the scene.
